texto: When your efforts run in the face of conventional wisdom and accepted mastery, persistence can look like madness. If you succeed in the end, this extreme originality reformulates into a new level of mastery, sometimes even genius; if you fail in the end, you remain a madman in the eyes of others, and maybe even yourself. When you are in the midst of the journey…there’s really no way of knowing which one you are.” (p.129)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hilary Austen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “When your efforts run in the face of conventional wisdom and accepted mastery, persistence can lo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, accepted-mastery, conventional-wisdom pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e termino.
